name: add_issue
description: Create a new support issue/ticket in the ERPClaw system. Use this when a customer reports a bug, feature request, complaint, question, or return request. Do not use this for warranty claims (use add-warranty-claim instead) or maintenance scheduling (use add-maintenance-schedule). Returns the new issue ID on success. Required: --subject. For priority use low, medium, high, or critical. For issue-type use bug, feature_request, question, complaint, or return.

subject.description: Issue subject/title (required). This is the short summary displayed in ticket lists.

customer_id.description: UUID of the customer reporting the issue. Obtain from existing customer data, do not invent.

item_id.description: UUID of the item/product related to the issue. Optional; omit if not applicable.

serial_number_id.description: UUID of the serial number for the item. Optional; used when tracking specific units.

priority.description: Issue priority. Defaults to medium if not specified. Use critical for production outages.

issue_type.description: Type of issue. Use bug for defects, feature_request for enhancements, question for inquiries, complaint for customer complaints, return for return requests.

description.description: Detailed description of the issue. Include steps to reproduce if applicable.

assigned_to.description: Employee UUID to assign the issue to. Obtain from existing employee data, do not invent.

sla_id.description: UUID of the SLA to apply. Obtain from list-slas; if not specified, default SLA is used if one exists.

name: update_issue
description: Update an existing support issue's fields. Use this to change status, reassign, update priority, or change issue type. Do not use this to resolve an issue (use resolve-issue) or reopen a closed issue (use reopen-issue). Closed issues cannot be updated; reopen first if needed. Required: --issue-id.

issue_id.description: UUID of the issue to update (required). Obtain from list-issues or get-issue.

status.description: New status. Use open for new issues, in_progress when working on it, waiting_on_customer when awaiting customer response, resolved when fixed, closed after resolution is confirmed. Do not change closed issues; use reopen-issue instead.

priority.description: New priority. Use critical for production outages.

assigned_to.description: Employee UUID to reassign the issue to. Obtain from existing employee data.

description.description: Updated description. Replaces the existing description entirely.

name: get_issue
description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific support issue by its UUID. Use this to view all details including status, priority, timestamps, customer info, comments, SLA applied, and breach status. Required: --issue-id. Returns all fields for the issue.

issue_id.description: UUID of the issue to retrieve (required). Obtain from list-issues.

name: list_issues
description: List and filter support issues from the ERPClaw system. Use this to view all tickets or filter by status, priority, customer, assignee, or company. Returns an array of issues with key details (id, subject, status, priority, customer_id, assigned_to, created_at). Use get-issue for full details on a specific issue.

status.description: Filter by status. Use open for unresolved issues, in_progress for active work, waiting_on_customer for pending customer response, resolved for fixed issues, closed for completed issues.

customer_id.description: Filter by customer UUID. Obtain from existing customer data.

company_id.description: Filter by company UUID. Filters through customer associations.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return. Default is typically 50.

offset.description: Pagination offset for results. Use with limit for paginated retrieval.

name: add_issue_comment
description: Add a comment or note to an existing support issue. Use this to add tracking notes, customer communications, or internal remarks. The first employee comment triggers SLA response time tracking. Do not use this to change issue status (use update-issue) or resolve (use resolve-issue). Required: --issue-id and --comment.

issue_id.description: UUID of the issue to add comment to (required). Obtain from list-issues or get-issue.

comment.description: Comment text (required). This is the content that will be recorded.

comment_by.description: Who is adding the comment. Use employee for internal notes or support responses, customer for customer-initiated comments.

is_internal.description: Internal comment flag. Set to 1 for internal notes not visible to customers, 0 for customer-visible comments. Defaults to 0.

name: resolve_issue
description: Mark a support issue as resolved with resolution notes. Use this when the issue has been fixed or completed. The system will check for SLA breach at this time - if SLA was breached, the breach flag remains set even after reopening. NOTE: This sets the resolved_at timestamp. Confirm with user before executing. Required: --issue-id.

issue_id.description: UUID of the issue to resolve (required). Obtain from list-issues or get-issue.

resolution_notes.description: Description of how the issue was resolved. Include root cause and solution for audit trail.

name: reopen_issue
description: Reopen a previously resolved or closed support issue. Use this when the issue resurfaces or was not fully resolved. Note: If SLA was breached before resolution, the breach flag remains set. Do not use this for new issues. Required: --issue-id. Confirm with user before executing.

issue_id.description: UUID of the issue to reopen (required). Obtain from list-issues or get-issue.

reason.description: Reason for reopening. Include why the issue was not fully resolved.

name: add_sla
description: Create a new Service Level Agreement (SLA) with response and resolution time targets by priority. Use this to define SLA policies with different response and resolution times for each priority level. Do not use this to apply an SLA to an issue (use add-issue with sla-id). Required: --name and --priorities JSON string.

name.description: SLA name (required). Use a descriptive name like 'Standard SLA' or 'Premium SLA'.

priorities.description: JSON string containing response_times and resolution_times by priority (required). Format: {"response_times": {"low": "48", "medium": "24", "high": "8", "critical": "4"}, "resolution_times": {"low": "120", "medium": "72", "high": "24", "critical": "8"}}. Values are hours as strings.

working_hours.description: Working hours configuration. Optional; specify as '9-5' or similar if non-standard.

is_default.description: Set as default SLA. Use '1' to make this the default for new issues without an explicit SLA.

name: list_slas
description: List all Service Level Agreements (SLAs) in the system. Use this to view available SLAs, their priority targets, and identify the default SLA. Returns an array of SLA objects with id, name, priorities, is_default. Use get-issue to see which SLA is applied to a specific issue.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

name: add_warranty_claim
description: File a new warranty claim for a customer's product. Use this when a customer claims warranty service for a product within their warranty period. Do not use this for general support issues (use add-issue). Required: --customer-id and --complaint-description.

customer_id.description: UUID of the customer filing the claim (required). Obtain from existing customer data.

complaint_description.description: Description of the complaint/defect (required). Include details about the issue and when it occurred.

item_id.description: UUID of the product/item. Obtain from existing item data.

serial_number_id.description: UUID of the serial number for the item being claimed. Obtain from serial number records.

warranty_expiry_date.description: Warranty expiry date in YYYY-MM-DD format. If not provided, the system may validate against existing warranty records.

name: update_warranty_claim
description: Update an existing warranty claim's status or resolution. Use this to change status, set resolution type, record resolution date, or update cost. Do not use this for general support issues. Required: --warranty-claim-id. NOTE: Confirm with user before updating resolution details.

warranty_claim_id.description: UUID of the warranty claim to update (required). Obtain from list-warranty-claims.

status.description: New status. Use pending for new claims, under_review when investigating, resolved when addressed, closed after completion.

resolution.description: Resolution type. Use repair for fixing, replace for replacement, refund for money back, rejected for denied claims.

resolution_date.description: Date of resolution in YYYY-MM-DD format.

cost.description: Resolution cost as decimal string (e.g., '150.00'). Stored as text.

name: list_warranty_claims
description: List and filter warranty claims in the system. Use this to view all warranty claims or filter by customer, status, or date range. Returns an array of claim objects with id, customer_id, status, resolution, created_at. Use get-issue for general support issues.

customer_id.description: Filter by customer UUID. Obtain from existing customer data.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

name: add_maintenance_schedule
description: Schedule recurring maintenance visits for customer equipment. Use this to set up periodic maintenance with specified frequency. Do not use this for one-off support issues (use add-issue). Required: --customer-id, --start-date, --end-date.

customer_id.description: UUID of the customer (required). Obtain from existing customer data.

start_date.description: Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format (required). When maintenance scheduling begins.

end_date.description: End date in YYYY-MM-DD format (required). When maintenance scheduling ends.

item_id.description: UUID of the item/equipment to maintain. Obtain from existing item data.

serial_number_id.description: UUID of the serial number for the item. Obtain from serial number records.

schedule_frequency.description: Maintenance frequency. Use monthly for every month, quarterly for every 3 months, semi_annual for twice yearly, annual for yearly.

assigned_to.description: Employee UUID assigned to perform maintenance. Obtain from existing employee data.

name: list_maintenance_schedules
description: List maintenance schedules in the system. Use this to view upcoming or past maintenance visits, filter by customer, item, or status. Returns an array of schedule objects with id, customer_id, start_date, end_date, frequency, status. Use record-maintenance-visit to log completed visits.

customer_id.description: Filter by customer UUID. Obtain from existing customer data.

status.description: Filter by status. Use scheduled for future visits, in_progress for active maintenance period, completed for finished schedules, cancelled for cancelled schedules.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

name: record_maintenance_visit
description: Log a completed maintenance visit. Use this to record that maintenance was performed, including observations, work done, and completion status. Do not use this to create a schedule (use add-maintenance-schedule). Required: --schedule-id and --visit-date.

schedule_id.description: UUID of the maintenance schedule (required). Obtain from list-maintenance-schedules.

visit_date.description: Date of the visit in YYYY-MM-DD format (required).

completed_by.description: Employee UUID who performed the visit. Obtain from existing employee data.

observations.description: Observations recorded during the visit. Include any issues noted or conditions observed.

work_done.description: Description of work performed during the visit. Include tasks completed and parts used.

status.description: Visit completion status. Use completed for fully completed, partial for partially completed, cancelled for cancelled visits.

name: sla_compliance_report
description: Generate an SLA compliance report showing service level agreement performance. Use this to review SLA breach rates, response times, and resolution times over a date range. Returns compliance metrics including breach percentages and average response/resolution times. Optional: --company-id, --from-date, --to-date. Use overdue-issues-report for specific overdue issue details.

company_id.description: Filter by company UUID. Filters through customer associations. Optional.

from_date.description: Start date for report range in YYYY-MM-DD format. Optional; defaults to 30 days ago if not specified.

to_date.description: End date for report range in YYYY-MM-DD format. Optional; defaults to today if not specified.

name: overdue_issues_report
description: Generate a report of overdue issues that have breached their SLA. Use this to identify critical issues requiring immediate attention. Returns a list of issues where SLA response or resolution time was exceeded. Use sla-compliance-report for overall compliance metrics.

company_id.description: Filter by company UUID. Filters through customer associations. Optional.

name: status
description: Get overall support status as a dashboard summary. Use this for a quick overview including open issues count, SLA compliance percentage, overdue issues count, and key metrics. Optional: --company-id filter. Use overdue-issues-report for specific overdue details, sla-compliance-report for detailed compliance.

company_id.description: Filter by company UUID. Returns status only for issues belonging to customers of this company. Optional.
